_comment: Canonical example for the REPO_PROFILE block emitted by the repo-discoverer agent (Sonnet) during /discover Phase B2.0. ONE file per repo at {run_dir}/outputs/repo-profiles/{repo-key}.json. The solution-architect (Opus) then reads the full set of profiles in Phase B2 to synthesize platform.md + audit-findings.md + the architecture diagrams. Field reference and consumer list live in block-schemas.md. Producers must match this structure; the architect extracts it via direct Read on the file. Stack-specific fields (under `stack` and `integrations`) are filled when relevant for the repo's role. The _comment field is illustrative only — strip it from real output.
description: Owns the editorial workflow for the ABVI catalogue. Accepts publisher submissions, drives each book through review → approval → publication, and manages the contracts that govern royalty terms. The authoritative source for Book and Contract state — every other service consumes its events or HTTP endpoints rather than reading its database directly.

entities.name: Book
entities.purpose: A publication moving through editorial review toward release.

entities.name: Publisher
entities.purpose: An author or organization that submits books.

entities.name: Contract
entities.purpose: The revenue agreement between a publisher and the platform for a given book.

outbound_events.purpose: Notify reviewers when a book is submitted

auth.enforcement_pattern: manual SecurityContextHolder reads in service layer (smell — see audit_findings)
role_decisions: Controllers use @PreAuthorize sparingly; some role checks happen in service

tests.harness: Tests use @SpringBootTest with full context (no @WebMvcTest pattern in this repo yet)

key_conventions: Service layer throws specific exceptions, mapped centrally by GlobalExceptionHandler
DTOs live under dto/ (not entities/) — request/response separated from JPA entities
MapStruct mappers under mapper/ for DTO ↔ entity translation
constraints_observed: Mixed authorization style — some @PreAuthorize, some manual SecurityContextHolder reads in services. Inconsistent and worth standardizing.

audit_findings.description: Token comparison uses == not equals() — fails for cached strings outside the constant pool
Logs raw user-supplied filename without sanitization (log injection risk)

notes_for_architect: This repo's auth pattern is inconsistent — flag in platform.md § Established Patterns or Known Constraints. The DTO/entity split convention is clean and worth elevating to workspace-wide pattern. Frontend repo references this service via `pms-frontend/src/api/publisher-api-specs.yaml`.
